Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer left the verbatim field blank or entered only non-meaningful characters.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer did not provide a verbatim response — field is blank empty whitespace-only or contains only punctuation. Include: '' 'n/a' 'na' 'none' 'nothing' 'no comment' 'no' '.' '-' '...' 'x' 'nope'. Exclude from all CX analysis. High volume — typically 30-40% of total surveys.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer left the verbatim field blank or entered only non-meaningful characters.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer entered a single word or very short phrase that provides no actionable insight. Include: 'good' 'great' 'fine' 'ok' 'okay' 'bad' 'terrible' 'yes' 'thanks' 'cool' 'whatever' 'sure' 'meh'. Note: Only classify here if the word provides NO topical signal — 'billing' alone would still route to Pricing.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer uses the verbatim to explain or correct the numeric score they gave — not to provide topical feedback.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer clarifies their numeric score or says they selected wrong. Include: 'I meant to give a 10' 'accidentally hit 1' 'my score should be higher' 'I gave a 9 not a 1' 'wrong button' 'meant to rate 5 stars' 'I hit the wrong number' 'thought 1 was the best'. Survey design signal — consider UI improvement.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer uses the verbatim to explain or correct the numeric score they gave — not to provide topical feedback.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer explains their high score with no actionable detail. Include: 'everything was perfect' 'no issues at all' 'couldn't be better' '10 out of 10' 'nothing to improve' 'all good' 'satisfied with everything' 'no complaints whatsoever'. Positive but non-actionable — exclude from driver analysis.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer uses the verbatim field to request removal from survey distribution or complain about survey frequency.
Sub_Topic: Stop Sending Surveys / Too Many Surveys
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer asks to be removed from survey list or complains about survey frequency. Include: 'stop sending me these' 'too many surveys' 'quit asking me' 'opt me out' 'unsubscribe' 'don't contact me' 'stop calling me for surveys' 'leave me alone' 'I already did this survey' 'stop emailing me'. Compliance signal — flag for suppression list.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Survey was sent to wrong person or customer is commenting on an interaction they did not have.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer says the survey does not apply to them or they did not have the interaction being asked about. Include: 'I never called' 'wasn't me' 'wrong person' 'I didn't contact AT&T' 'this wasn't my experience' 'someone else used my account' 'never visited the store' 'I didn't chat with anyone'. Survey targeting signal.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Response contains random characters keyboard mashing or text that cannot be interpreted as meaningful feedback.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Response is gibberish random characters or clearly unintentional input. Include: 'asdfgh' 'jjjjjj' 'qwerty' '123456' 'hhhhhh' 'test test' random emoji strings accidental dictation noise. Exclude from all analysis.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer responded in a language other than English — may contain valid feedback but requires translation before classification.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer provided feedback in Spanish French or another non-English language. Include: any response primarily in a non-English language. Do NOT auto-classify as negative — may contain positive or negative feedback. Flag for translation pipeline then re-classify. Common in markets with high Spanish-speaking populations.

Category_Definition: Responses that do not contain actionable customer feedback — including blank responses gibberish wrong-customer captures score clarifications opt-out requests and foreign language entries. Used for data hygiene and exclusion from CX analytics.
Topic_Definition: Customer complains about the survey itself — its length design or timing — rather than providing feedback about AT&T service.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer complains about the survey experience rather than providing AT&T service feedback. Include: 'this survey is too long' 'too many questions' 'why so many questions' 'survey doesn't apply' 'questions don't make sense' 'survey is confusing' 'took too long' 'fix your survey'. Survey design signal — distinct from opt-out requests.
